document.title: TRUMP'S most CHAOTIC press conference ending has everyone SPEECHLESS

transcript.text: Mr. President, you voiced your displeasure with NATO in the past. Is there a danger to the US not being the de facto of the leader of the alliance and then other powers within the alliance then getting the decision-making when it comes to wars and nuclear weapons? it's not a danger. NATO's Look, we went to NATO. I didn't ask very strongly. I just said, "Hey, if you want to help, great." No, no, no, we will not help. I said, "That's all right. You don't want to help." Cuz I've always said NATO is a paper tiger. See, NATO is a paper tiger. Putin's not afraid of NATO. Putin's afraid of us, very afraid of us. And he's explained it to me a lot of times. I got to know him very well. I know him very well. Uh NATO is a paper tiger. NATO is us. And when we needed them, but we didn't need them, by the way. We didn't need them, obviously, cuz they haven't helped at all. Just the opposite. They've actually gone out of their way not to help. They didn't even want to give us landing strips. Think of it. And it's not just NATO. You know who else didn't help us? South Korea didn't help us. You know who else didn't help us? Australia didn't help us. You know who else didn't help us? Japan. We've got 50,000 soldiers in Japan to protect them from North Korea. We have 45,000 soldiers in South Korea to protect us from Kim Jong-un, who I get along with very well, as you know. Do you notice he said very nice things about me? He used to call uh Joe Biden a mentally person, okay? So, don't tell me about your stuff. Joe Biden, he said he's a mentally person. He was so nasty to Joe Biden. It was terrible. But to me, he likes Trump. And do you notice how nice things are with North Korea? It's very nice. But we have 45,000 people soldiers in harm's way and right next to Kim Jong-un with a lot of nuclear weapons. 45 which should have never happened if a certain president, I'm not going to mention this president cuz I happen to like him, believe it or not. But if a certain president did his job, Kim Jong-un would not have nuclear weapons right now. But they're all afraid to do their job properly. But just to conclude and just to finish, Japan didn't help us, Australia didn't help us, South Korea didn't help us. And then you get to NATO, NATO didn't help us. There were some countries that did. Now, countries that have been good, now you could also say they're got to be a little bit more involved because they're in the territory. But Saudi Arabia's been excellent. Qatar's been excellent. UAE has been excellent. Bahrain, Kuwait, I mean, Kuwait did shoot down three of our planes. The only planes really that we lost were friendly fire, they called it. I call it unfriendly fire. They unfortunately didn't know how to use our our great Patriots. The pilots said, "What kind of a missile's coming at us?" Patriot. Boom. They got out. Because they know Patriot never misses. So, they had beautiful Patriots. There were planes heading in their direction. Unfortunately, they decided to shoot those planes. They were our planes. So, No, NATO is a paper tiger. Now, he's coming to see me on Wednesday, as you know. He's a wonderful guy. Secretary General is great. And Mark Rutte is a great person, but he's got And you know, it all began with, if you want to know the truth, Greenland. We want Greenland. They don't want to give it to us and I said, "Bye-bye." Okay, thank you very much, everybody. Thank you. What about rising gas prices for Americans? What are your concerns? General

Is there a danger to the US not being the de facto of the leader of the alliance and then other powers within the alliance then getting the decision-making when it comes to wars and nuclear weapons? it's not a danger. NATO's Look, we went to NATO. I didn't ask very strongly. I just said, "Hey, if you want to help, great." No, no, no, we will not help. I said, "That's all right. You don't want to help." Cuz I've always said NATO is a paper tiger. See, NATO is a paper tiger. Putin's not afraid of NATO. Putin's afraid of us, very afraid of us. And he's explained it to me a lot of times. I got to know him very well. I know him very well. Uh NATO is a paper tiger. NATO is us. And when we needed them, but we didn't need them, by the way. We didn't need them, obviously, cuz they haven't helped at all. Just the opposite. They've actually gone out of their way not to help. They didn't even want to give us landing strips. Think of it. chunks.speaker: Mr. President

summary.short_summary: The speaker criticizes NATO's effectiveness, stating that it is a 'paper tiger' and that other alliance members did not help during a hypothetical scenario involving Putin and other countries like South Korea, Japan, and Australia.
summary.long_summary: The speaker expresses skepticism about NATO's ability to act effectively, referring to it as a 'paper tiger' and suggesting that Putin does not fear the alliance but rather the U.S. The speaker recounts his personal interactions with Putin, stating that he knows Putin well and that Putin is very afraid of the U.S. The speaker also criticizes other alliance members, such as South Korea, Japan, and Australia, for not helping during a hypothetical scenario involving Putin. The speaker notes that these countries did not offer landing strips or other assistance and implies that they have gone out of their way not to help.

glossary.definition: An alliance of North Atlantic countries, including the United States, designed for collective defense.
An entity that appears to be strong or significant but is in reality weak or ineffective.
The Russian President referred to in the conversation.
The leader of North Korea mentioned in the conversation.

key_points.point: There is no danger to the US if it is not the de facto leader of NATO and other powers make decisions regarding wars and nuclear weapons.
NATO is described as a paper tiger and not a threat to Putin.
Putin is not afraid of NATO, but he is very afraid of the US.
NATO did not help when needed, and other countries like South Korea, Australia, and Japan also did not assist.
There are 50,000 soldiers in Japan and 45,000 in South Korea for protection against North Korea and Kim Jong-un respectively.
Kim Jong-un used to call Joe Biden a mentally person.

quiz.question: What does the speaker imply about NATO's effectiveness in times of need?
quiz.answer: NATO is a paper tiger and has not helped during times when the speaker needed assistance.

chunks.text: We've got 50,000 soldiers in Japan to protect them from North Korea. We have 45,000 soldiers in South Korea to protect us from Kim Jong-un, who I get along with very well, as you know. Do you notice he said very nice things about me? He used to call uh Joe Biden a mentally person, okay? So, don't tell me about your stuff. Joe Biden, he said he's a mentally person. He was so nasty to Joe Biden. It was terrible. But to me, he likes Trump. And do you notice how nice things are with North Korea? It's very nice. But we have 45,000 people soldiers in harm's way and right next to Kim Jong-un with a lot of nuclear weapons. 45 which should have never happened if a certain president, I'm not going to mention this president cuz I happen to like him, believe it or not. But if a certain president did his job, Kim Jong-un would not have nuclear weapons right now. But they're all afraid to do their job properly. But just to conclude and just to finish, Japan didn't help us, Australia didn't help us, South Korea didn't help us. And then you get to NATO, NATO didn't help us. There were some countries that did. Now, countries that have been good, now you could also say they're got to be a little bit more involved because they're in the territory. But Saudi Arabia's been excellent. Qatar's been excellent. UAE has been excellent. Bahrain, Kuwait, I mean, Kuwait did shoot down three of our planes. The only planes really that we lost were friendly fire, they called it. I call it unfriendly fire.
chunks.speaker: unspecified (third-person perspective)

summary.short_summary: The text discusses the number of soldiers stationed in Japan and South Korea, the relationship with North Korea's leader, and criticizes previous leaders for not stopping North Korea's nuclear program.
summary.long_summary: The text provides a detailed critique of the current and past military strategies and diplomatic relations with North Korea. It mentions that there are 50,000 soldiers in Japan and 45,000 in South Korea, positioning them to protect against North Korea. The speaker expresses a good relationship with North Korea's leader, Kim Jong-un, and mentions that North Korea has been very nice towards this unnamed individual. The text also criticizes a previous president for not having stopped North Korea from acquiring nuclear weapons. The speaker is frustrated with allies and NATO for not providing support and instead praises countries like Saudi Arabia, Qatar, UAE, and Bahrain for their assistance.

glossary.definition: The leader of North Korea mentioned in the text who has been described in a negative light by the speaker.
The current President of the United States mentioned in the text who is described negatively by the speaker.
A reference to former U.S. President Donald Trump, who is positively compared to Joe Biden by the speaker.
A country mentioned as having been excellent in some way, though the context is not clear.
A country mentioned as having been excellent in some way, though the context is not clear.
Abbreviation for United Arab Emirates, a country mentioned as having been excellent in some way, though the context is not clear.
A country mentioned as having been excellent in some way, though the context is not clear.
A country mentioned for shooting down three of the speaker's planes, though the speaker disputes this characterization.

key_points.point: We have 50,000 soldiers in Japan to protect from North Korea.
We have 45,000 soldiers in South Korea to protect from Kim Jong-un.
Kim Jong-un used to call Joe Biden a mentally person.
Kim Jong-un likes Trump and North Korea is very nice now.
There are 45,000 soldiers in harm's way with a lot of nuclear weapons.
A certain president should have prevented Kim Jong-un from having nuclear weapons.
Japan, Australia, South Korea, and NATO did not help.
Saudi Arabia, Qatar, UAE, and Bahrain have been excellent.
Kuwait shot down three of our planes.
